title: Waitomo Glowworm Caves at Midnight â New Zealand
prompt: A breathtaking underground cave system in Waitomo, New Zealand, photographed at midnight. The cave ceiling is blanketed with thousands of tiny bioluminescent glowworms (Arachnocampa luminosa), casting an ethereal, deep blue-cyan glow across the still dark water below, creating a perfect mirror reflection of the starlike ceiling. Ancient limestone formations â stalactites and flowstones â are softly lit in cool blue and teal tones. A small wooden boat drifts silently through the subterranean river, adding scale to the immense cavern. The atmosphere is magical, otherworldly, and serene. Ultra-detailed, photorealistic, long-exposure photography aesthetic, deep blacks, glowing cyan bioluminescence, perfectly still reflective water.
